title: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Failure
description: Troubleshooting MFA failures where the verification code or push notification does not work.

steps.title: Sync Your Phone's Time Setting
steps.instruction:
*🕐 Step 1: Sync Your Phone's Time Setting*
• MFA codes are time-based — if your phone clock is even slightly off, the code will be rejected.
• On Android: *Settings → General Management → Date and Time → Automatic Date and Time → ON*.
• On iPhone: *Settings → General → Date & Time → Set Automatically → ON*.
• After syncing, open your authenticator app and try the new code.
_Expected: Time syncs and authenticator codes match correctly._

steps.title: Check Phone Internet Connection
steps.instruction:
*📶 Step 2: Check Phone Internet Connection (Push Notifications)*
• If your MFA uses push notifications (like Microsoft Authenticator approve/deny), ensure your phone has:
  - An active Wi-Fi or Mobile Data connection.
  - Notifications enabled for the Authenticator app.
• Go to *Phone Settings → Notifications → Authenticator App → Allow Notifications*.
_Expected: Push notification arrives on your phone for approval._

steps.title: Use the Manual Code Instead
steps.instruction:
*🔢 Step 3: Use the Manual 6-Digit Code*
• Instead of waiting for a push notification, open your Authenticator app manually.
• Find your account entry and use the *6-digit time-based code* shown.
• Enter it quickly — codes expire every 30 seconds. Tap/click the code to copy if available.
_Expected: Manual code entry works even if push notifications are delayed._

steps.title: Update or Reinstall the Authenticator App
steps.instruction:
*📲 Step 4: Update or Reinstall the Authenticator App*
• Open your phone's App Store (iOS) or Google Play Store (Android).
• Search for your Authenticator app (Microsoft Authenticator / Google Authenticator / Zoho OneAuth).
• If an update is available, install it.
• If the issue persists, reinstall the app — but *backup your accounts first* if the app supports it.
_Expected: Updated or fresh app functions correctly._

steps.title: Raise an MFA Reset Ticket
steps.instruction:
*🎫 Step 5: Raise an MFA Reset Ticket with IT*
• If you lost your phone, changed your phone number, or cannot access your authenticator app:
• Raise a ticket with IT requesting an *MFA Reset*.
• Include in your ticket:
  - Your Employee ID
  - Your registered mobile number
  - The account/app that you cannot access (e.g., Microsoft 365, Zoho Mail)
• IT will verify your identity and reset your MFA so you can re-enroll.
_Expected: IT resets MFA and sends re-enrollment instructions._
